title: Convert Scanned Receipts to Excel: Complete OCR Workflow Guide
metaTitle: Convert Scanned Receipts to Excel with OCR | pdfexcel.ai
metaDescription: Turn scanned paper receipts into structured Excel spreadsheets using OCR technology. Extract vendor, amount, date, and tax data automatically.
h1: Convert Scanned Receipts to Excel Using OCR Technology

hero.headline: Transform Paper Receipts into Excel Spreadsheets
hero.subheadline: Use OCR technology to extract vendor names, amounts, dates, and tax information from scanned receipts and organize them in structured Excel files.

summary: Converting scanned receipts to Excel involves using OCR technology to extract key data fields like vendor names, purchase amounts, transaction dates, and tax information from physical receipts, then organizing this data into structured spreadsheet rows for expense tracking and accounting purposes.
whenThisIsRelevant: Preparing tax documentation from paper receipts
Digitizing years of accumulated paper receipts
expectedOutputs: Excel files with vendor, amount, date columns
CSV spreadsheets ready for accounting software import
commonChallenges: Faded or low-quality receipt images producing incomplete data
Thermal receipts that have degraded over time
Receipts with non-standard layouts requiring field customization
Mixed currencies and tax rates in international receipts
howItWorksSteps: Upload scanned receipt images or PDF files to the OCR platform
Select which data fields to extract (vendor, amount, date, tax, items)
Run OCR processing to convert image text into structured data
Download the resulting Excel file with one row per receipt
whyPdfExcelAiFits: OCR technology specifically handles scanned documents and photos
Custom field selection lets you choose relevant receipt data points
Batch processing handles multiple receipts simultaneously
99%+ accuracy on clear receipt images reduces manual corrections
limitations: Accuracy depends on receipt image quality and legibility
Handwritten receipts may require manual review for accuracy
Very faded thermal receipts may have missing or incomplete data

faq.question: What receipt data can be extracted into Excel columns?
faq.answer: Common fields include vendor name, transaction date, total amount, tax amount, payment method, and individual line items depending on receipt clarity and format.

faq.question: Do phone photos of receipts work as well as scanned images?
faq.answer: Phone photos work well when taken with good lighting and focus. However, scanner-quality images typically produce higher OCR accuracy rates.

faq.question: Can faded thermal receipts be processed accurately?
faq.answer: Thermal receipts that have faded significantly may produce incomplete results. The OCR accuracy depends heavily on how much text remains visible in the image.

faq.question: How many receipts can be processed in a single batch?
faq.answer: The platform supports batch processing of multiple receipt images simultaneously, with each receipt becoming a separate row in the output Excel file.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
Pay-as-you-go pricing with free starter option
99%+ accuracy rate on clear document images
Supports multiple image formats and scanned PDFs
exampleUseCases: Restaurant owner digitizing daily cash register receipts
Consultant organizing client meal receipts for reimbursement
Small business preparing quarterly tax filings from paper receipts
Property manager tracking maintenance and supply receipts
